title: General Paratransit/Community Ride Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Transportation > General Paratransit/Community Ride Programs 6 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation for medical appointments, personal business, and shopping. Priority is given to life-sustaining medical appointments. Rides are provided within Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties. The Ride Together program allows riders to recruit their own volunteer driver (i.e., family, neighbors, and friends). Participants schedule rides with their volunteer driver. Mileage reimbursement is provided, and volunteer training is required. The Vets Driving Vets program matches veterans who volunteer their services with other veterans who need rides to doctors' appointments or to take care of other basic needs. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Age 65 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Transportation services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 655-8208 3 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLEARVIEW DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER TRANSPORTATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers private and non-emergency medical transportation services. 1114 SW Frazer Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Low-income households, adults with disabilities, or age 55 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 276-1130 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DIAL-A-BUS DEMAND RESPONSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportation for individuals who need a ride for any reason. 4077 SW Research Way Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Adults 18 and older who do not qualify for Benton Area Transit Lift Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5:30pm. Rides may be provided outside of these hours. Follows the Corvallis School District inclement weather closure policy and closed on federal holidays. Email (541) 752-2615 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GILLIAM COUNTY SENIOR SERVICES GILLIAM COUNTY TRANSPORTATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transportion to medical appointments, shopping, social, or business trips, with medical being the priority. 615 E Cottonwood Lane Condon, OR 97823 Eligibility: Unrestricted; age 60 and older and people with disabilities are prioritized Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Transportation available Monday-Friday, and occasional weekends Email (541) 351-9545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORROW COUNTY PUBLIC TRANSIT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides public transit options. Eligibility: Morrow County residents Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Ride hours vary by destination Email (541) 676-5667 541-676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION TRANSIT AND TAXI SERVICE FINDER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an online search tool to help locate local bus services, shuttle services, taxi services, Dial-a-Ride, and long distance bus travel. 355 Capitol Street NE MS 11 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable 511 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES TRANSPORTATION REACHING PEOPLE CLEARVIEW DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER TRANSPORTATION DIAL-A-BUS DEMAND RESPONSE GILLIAM COUNTY SENIOR SERVICES GILLIAM COUNTY TRANSPORTATION OREGON DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION TRANSIT AND TAXI SERVICE FINDER
